title: Reis Poem
wikitext:
[]: Rei's Poem             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Jun 15, 2010 11:48 pm Recent discussions on Episode #14 lead me to the current wiki page on Rei's monologue , written largely by Zugzwang. Since I found myself in disagreement with the general conclusions there, I thought I'd take the opportunity to offer my own interpretations of Rei's Monologue in this episode and invite other interpretations and discussions of it. EDIT: Changed the description from "Rei's Poem" to Rei's monologue on Reichu's suggestion. Since I believe the visuals of the monologue are almost as important as the words spoken, I'll add them alongside the relevant lines and comments. First, for reference, this is the complete "poem" Rei's Monologue  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Jun 15, 2010 11:49 pm [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_16.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_17.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_18.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_19.jpg[/wkimg] Who is this? This is me. Who am I? What am I? What am I? What am I? What am I? These lines point to Rei having overall a very odd experience in the entry plug. She asks, " Who is this? ", answering " This is me. ". This points to Rei having something of an out of body experience, probably an inevitable result of syncing with the Eva. She is seeing or sensing herself form the outside and must now reaffirm herself. The link here to her nature as one of many clones is clear. Of note here is the "red space" background seen behind the Rei instances, again perhaps hinting at creation, but perhaps also hinting at the connection being made to the Evas core. [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_18.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_19.jpg[/wkimg] I am myself. This object is me. The form that shapes me. This is the me that can be seen, yet I feel as though I am not myself. Very strange. I feel as if my body is melting. I can no longer see myself. My shape is fading. Continuing the notion of Rei having an out of body experience, we have her again reaffirming her own form. This entire sequence could be viewed as giving context to the monologue as a whole with Rei, quasi-disembodied as a result of the synch, faced in a very direct way with the nature of existence in general, and hers in particular! Her words about "not feeling herself", feeling as if she is "melting", and her shape "fading" could all hint at how the link to Unit-01 is affecting her, either mentally or perhaps even physically. She could be recalling her origins, or resonating through them with Unit-01 which contains the soul of Yui, Rei's "blueprint", and whose body was also extracted from Lilith's. Her comment about "melting" brings to mind tangification and Shinji's experiences in #20, #25, and #26', and Yui's absorption into Unit-01. Perhaps Rei is in some slight danger here, as Shinji is later in the episode? I also note in passing that as Rei's silhouette floats down, the sun enters the shot; Perhaps not signifying anything in particular. [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_19.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_20.jpg[/wkimg] I feel the presence of someone who is not me. Is someone there, beyond this? These lines represent Rei's first awareness of the entity in Unit-01, Yui, and give context to the rest of the monologue which follows. What's important to note here is that Rei asks the question " Is someone there..? ", indicating that she was not aware that there would be "someone else" in Unit-01. How can this be if she is clearly aware of the existence of an other in each Eva, as indicated by her conversation with Asuka in #22' ( "Yes, the Evas have souls...You should know that." ). The answer to this paradox is found in the first line. " The presence of someone who is not me ", which implies that the soul in Unit-00 "is me"(See her line in #23' " the me inside the Eva "). In other words, Rei must have been expecting that the soul in Unit-01 was like the one in Unit-00, another "me" or shard of Lilith, and was not expecting to find Yui—a wholly different entity. This may account for the odd experience she is having. This recognition gives an interesting context to the lines which now follow. [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_21.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_22.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_23.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_24.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_25.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_26.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_27.jpg[/wkimg] Ikari-kun? I know this person. Major Katsuragi. Doctor Akagi. Everyone. Classmates. The pilot of Unit 02. Commander Ikari? My reading of these lines and the images that accompany them is that they essentially represent an inquiry by Unit-01, that is by Yui, who is taking this opportunity to ask Rei questions in an effort to names to faces. Rei is first either reminded of Shinji or his feelings, or else confuses the presence in the Eva with him owing to the images which now follow (The use of "Ikari" here may be an instance of irony) . The next line truly reveals what's going on. " I know this person. Major Katsuragi. " This line shows that Misato has not simply popped into Rei's thoughts here. Her image has come to Rei from "outside", that is from the Eva and without a name to it. Rei is presented with a face without a name, and proceeds to put one to it. In support of this point, we have Rei now proceed to put more names to faces, but importantly, they are Rei's names for these people and not Shinji's. "Major Katsuragi" and not "Misato-san". "Dr. Akagi" and not "Ritsuko-san". "Unit-02's pilot" and not "Asuka". My hypothesis here is that Yui, in a quasi-conscious existence in Unit-01, has been obtaining impressions of the people in Shinji's life from him but has no way of putting names to them. Rei's arrival presents an opportunity to learn about Shinji and the people his life from someone else. I'd like to hear what people's thoughts on this are? Is it likely that Yui can/would do this? Is there another plausible explanation for this series of images and names? The last line in this "standard" inquiry sequence is "Commander Ikari?", for which we are presented not an image of Gendo from Shinji's perspective, but one from Rei's. While this could be taken as evidence against the hypothesis above, my reading of this is that the image both simultaneously represents how distant Gendo is in Shinji's mind, and also how much Rei associates her glasses keepsake with Gendo. Recalling that the commander is something "comforting" for Rei, but cold and harsh for Shinji, this image may have dual meaning. However, the interrogation is perhaps not over just yet. [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_28.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_29.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_30.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_31.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_32.jpg[/wkimg] Who are you? Who are you? Who are you? Assuming the above hypothesis, there is still one person who has been left out of this game of "name that face"; that is, Rei herself. And this would have implications for the "repeated" lines here. The first "Who are you?" is asked over an image of Rei herself. It's the OP image of Rei, in her apartment , probably from the perspective of her mirror , looking out from the mirror (which would make this sequence very clever visually if it was the case) . It could perhaps be assumed that (see below) this "Who are you?" is intended to be in the same vein as the ones before, but something is about to go awry with the questioning. The next shots are from episode #02, when Shinji sees the Eva's "true face" . Recall that in this sequence, Shinji was in fact looking at the Eva's reflection in the glass of a building . Shinji saw the Eva's eye, seeing his eye, and saw his eye through the Eva's eyes seeing his own eye, leading to his understandable scream. We have something like that sequence played out again here, only this time in a far more disorienting and mind blowing fashion. The second "Who are you?"(over the Eva face) is probably Rei "seeing" the Eva and returning the question, only to have it returned or echoed back once again(Over the eye shots). This is the final question, and the final part of the monologue, probably because something goes horribly existentially wrong the moment Yui asks Rei who Rei is, bringing the whole thread of the monologue to a philosophical train wreck of a conclusion. Yui, who has faces but not names, ask's Rei who the person who looks like "me"(Yui) is. But is the question "Who are you?" or "Who is me?". To which Rei must then seek clarification with "Who are you?", which raises almost exactly the same problems, and so the line repeats. The following sentence is false. The previous sentence is true. Yui's question here probably leads to much the same mental paradox, albeit in a very personal fashion for all concerned. Like trying to guide your actions by looking at your reflection, only your reflection is now a completely different entity and is asking you questions. Akin Shinji's scream of terror when faced with being his reflection staring back at him, when faced with her reflection thinking back at her Rei is startled out of her philosophical musings (In a rare display of surprise), and is brought back to "reality" in the plug. [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_33.jpg[/wkimg] -------------------------------------- That concludes these thoughts on the monologue and its meanings. It was a lot, but I hope it has been worth your time to read. I'd be delighted to hear some options and alternative views on this. I think this monologue has long been denied the in depth debate it so richly deserves. EDIT: That earlier "her her" above was supposed to have read "her mirror". EDIT: "Poem" changed to monologue, though the image file names remain unaltered.

[]:             Quote Post by SleepS » Wed Jun 16, 2010 2:02 am Nice reading, OMF! :) However, question: for your theory that Yui is presenting images to of people in Shinji's life. Don't you think the particular order in which these images is displayed sparks a hint of importance? And if in fact, they were images Yui had seen through Shinji's subconscious, don't you think Asuka's picture would have come before his Touji's, Kensukes, and Hikari's? Shinji probably has invested more time thinking about Asuka than his classmates and everybody else, no? That aside, I thoroughly enjoyed your interpretation. I'll re-read it tomorrow, when I'm not so sleepy.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Wed Jun 16, 2010 2:08 am Actually, it makes sense from a "prying mother" angle. First she asks about the adults, those women who are Shinji's reach and are thus only interesting in the "Who are they?" sense. Then she brings up the teenage boys, his best friends. Note that the scene is the scene where they tease him for looking at Rei. Perhaps she's asking "Are these boys good friends for my son?" Then the girls come up; first Hikari, relegated to the periphery of Shinji's thoughts but still worth noting; then Asuka, the girl he seems to be LIVING with.

[]:             Quote Post by SleepS » Wed Jun 16, 2010 5:39 am Makes sense to me. :)

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed Jun 16, 2010 5:51 am The thing about most interpretations that always bugs me is when they omnislash a set of lines from the show into atomic parts then analyze each of those in isolation from the others. (I have thought so ever since first disagreeing with the common interpretation of Ritsuko's Reiquarium speech.) But this interpretation presents a consistent whole. At least the part about Yui inquiring, which is the part that has my attention.

[]:             Quote Post by Eudon » Wed Jun 16, 2010 6:40 am That was a very interesting reading, therefore thank you for the effort in assembling this post. Your interpretation makes sense to me anyway, I agree with your prespective.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Jun 16, 2010 9:54 am SleepS wrote: Don't you think the particular order in which these images is displayed sparks a hint of importance? And if in fact, they were images Yui had seen through Shinji's subconscious, don't you think Asuka's picture would have come before his Touji's, Kensukes, and Hikari's?

[]: Let's contemplate our navels for a minute here.             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Wed Jun 16, 2010 11:48 am I should've gotten in the shower thirty minutes ago, but once I started reading the analysis I couldn't stop. Very interesting read. The only thing that troubles me a bit is the 'order of importance' of the images Yui shows Rei. I understand that Rei and Asuka's personalities are rather unfathomable to Shinji, but them being two girls his own age that he spends more time with than anyone else, you'd think they'd rank a bit higher on the list. Even if Shinji doesn't try to understand Asuka, he does frequently question Rei and tries to understand her motives. Unless the 'importance' is actually in what the images represent , and not the depicted people themselves . For example, Misato represents The Mother and would thus rank higher in importance to that of Hikari, who represents School.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jun 16, 2010 11:49 am Still waking up, so I can say at the moment is that it's a monologue, not a poem. Please, please don't call it a poem. Rei is not writing poetry while she's tripping inside Eva-01, she's monologuing. If this pet peeve is completely substanceless, please tell me so.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Wed Jun 16, 2010 12:05 pm I'll examine this more closely a little later, but this popped into my head first: You should try to find a way to reenforce your idea that "unneeded sunflowers" = excess Rei clones by refering to the sunflower image from the infamous Reiquarium images . That sunflowers are referenced in the scene where the clones are destroyed should be significant. [wkimg width=200]File:Tank image 19.jpg[/wkimg]

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Wed Jun 16, 2010 12:06 pm This thread is a testament to why I love this place. OMF-san ... you have given me much to read! I did always get the sense of some sort of "dialogue" between Yui and whatever aspect of Lilith makes up "Rei". (By "dialogue" I mean something more subtle than actual speech, but as an anime we sort of need somebody to "talk".) When I see the shot of Eva's crazy eye looking at the cockpit, I think of Yui taking a good look at Lilith. Due to my Catholicesque background, I think of people as "body" and "soul", I always think of Lilith as having a physical aspect (which is where Unit-01 comes from) and a metaphysical aspect (the presence residing in the shell called "Rei"). I know it is more complicated than that, but that's sort of my instinctive reaction. So when I think of Rei, I tend to think of her as a confused Lilith. So a confused Lilith conversing with a crafty Yui. Is that completely off-base? I do have to consider this more before replying further. ^^; Reichu wrote: it's a monologue, not a poem

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jun 16, 2010 12:28 pm The sun > water > flowers thing is interesting as a comment on Rei's "water and light". It does seem rather out of place when that line usually seems to refer to a reflection of the moon on water, but in combination with the sunfrlowers bit I think it works as a parallel. The pertinent part of the thread I mentioned: I wrote: Dogen Zenji wrote: Enlightenment is like the moon reflected on the water. The moon does not get wet, nor is the water broken. Although its light is wide and great, the moon is reflected even in a puddle an inch wide. The whole moon and the entire sky are reflected in dewdrops on the grass, or even in one drop of water. Enlightenment does not divide you, just as the moon does not break the water. You cannot hinder enlightenment, just as a drop of water does not hinder the moon in the sky. The depth of the drop is the height of the moon. Each reflection, however long or short its duration, manifests the vastness of the dewdrop, and realizes the limitlessness of the moonlight in the sky.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Jun 16, 2010 1:37 pm Reichu wrote: If this pet peeve is completely substanceless, please tell me so.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed Jun 16, 2010 5:06 pm Then again, perhaps as you say, the image associated with Rei is that of a reflection of the moon. With Lilith as the moon and Rei as being merely the reflection. I'm unsure of how far these parallels can realistically be drawn.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Jun 16, 2010 8:03 pm Well, that and Lilith is the reason the moon EXISTS, and Rei is a reflection of that force.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Jun 16, 2010 11:55 pm Amazing job, OMF. FWIW, I agree with pretty much everything, but since I'm tight on time tonight I don't have time to respond sufficiently. But, on this: Reichu wrote: I can say at the moment is that it's a monologue, not a poem... If this pet peeve is completely substanceless, please tell me so.

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Thu Jun 17, 2010 7:29 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: I'm glad I'm not the only one to notice this potential dialogue. But wat is your thinking on the notion of Yui's questions about putting names to faces?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Jun 17, 2010 9:52 pm God's soul

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Jun 18, 2010 12:58 am I was under the impression the order of people from most to least important/close is Rei's POV as per Zuggy's thoughts. She's closest to Shinji, yet barely knows Gendo and even says so in one episode. Somewhat similiar to Asuka's "who are you?" mindrape where the Asuka mockers are (sorry if I messed up the order) Misato, Hikari, Rei, Maya, Ritsuko in terms of seiyuu. Also, old debate but why must Rei's "woman who never bleeds" line be about herself? Can't it be dual-meaning with Lilith's LCL?

Article: Reis Poem
[]: Rei's Poem             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Jun 15, 2010 11:48 pm Recent discussions on Episode #14 lead me to the current wiki page on Rei's monologue , written largely by Zugzwang. Since I found myself in disagreement with the general conclusions there, I thought I'd take the opportunity to offer my own interpretations of Rei's Monologue in this episode and invite other interpretations and discussions of it. EDIT: Changed the description from "Rei's Poem" to Rei's monologue on Reichu's suggestion. Since I believe the visuals of the monologue are almost as important as the words spoken, I'll add them alongside the relevant lines and comments. First, for reference, this is the complete "poem" Rei's Monologue  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Jun 15, 2010 11:49 pm [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_16.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_17.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_18.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_19.jpg[/wkimg] Who is this? This is me. Who am I? What am I? What am I? What am I? What am I? These lines point to Rei having overall a very odd experience in the entry plug. She asks, " Who is this? ", answering " This is me. ". This points to Rei having something of an out of body experience, probably an inevitable result of syncing with the Eva. She is seeing or sensing herself form the outside and must now reaffirm herself. The link here to her nature as one of many clones is clear. Of note here is the "red space" background seen behind the Rei instances, again perhaps hinting at creation, but perhaps also hinting at the connection being made to the Evas core. [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_18.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_19.jpg[/wkimg] I am myself. This object is me. The form that shapes me. This is the me that can be seen, yet I feel as though I am not myself. Very strange.

Article: Reis Poem
]14_rei_poem_19.jpg[/wkimg] I am myself. This object is me. The form that shapes me. This is the me that can be seen, yet I feel as though I am not myself. Very strange. I feel as if my body is melting. I can no longer see myself. My shape is fading. Continuing the notion of Rei having an out of body experience, we have her again reaffirming her own form. This entire sequence could be viewed as giving context to the monologue as a whole with Rei, quasi-disembodied as a result of the synch, faced in a very direct way with the nature of existence in general, and hers in particular! Her words about "not feeling herself", feeling as if she is "melting", and her shape "fading" could all hint at how the link to Unit-01 is affecting her, either mentally or perhaps even physically. She could be recalling her origins, or resonating through them with Unit-01 which contains the soul of Yui, Rei's "blueprint", and whose body was also extracted from Lilith's. Her comment about "melting" brings to mind tangification and Shinji's experiences in #20, #25, and #26', and Yui's absorption into Unit-01. Perhaps Rei is in some slight danger here, as Shinji is later in the episode? I also note in passing that as Rei's silhouette floats down, the sun enters the shot; Perhaps not signifying anything in particular. [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_19.jpg[/wkimg] [wkimg width=200]14_rei_poem_20.jpg[/wkimg] I feel the presence of someone who is not me. Is someone there, beyond this? These lines represent Rei's first awareness of the entity in Unit-01, Yui, and give context to the rest of the monologue which follows. What's important to note here is that Rei asks the question " Is someone there..? ", indicating that she was not aware that there would be "someone else" in Unit-01. How can this be if she is clearly aware of the existence of an other in each Eva, as indicated by her conversation with Asuka in #22' ( "Yes, the Evas have souls...You should know that." ). The answer to this paradox is found in the first line. " The presence of someone who is not me ", which implies that the

In other words, Rei must have been expecting that the soul in Unit-01 was like the one in Unit-00, another "me" or shard of Lilith, and was not expecting to find Yui—a wholly different entity. This may account for the odd experience she is having. This recognition gives an interesting context to the lines which now follow. My reading of these lines and the images that accompany them is that they essentially represent an inquiry by Unit-01, that is by Yui, who is taking this opportunity to ask Rei questions in an effort to names to faces. Rei is first either reminded of Shinji or his feelings, or else confuses the presence in the Eva with him owing to the images which now follow (The use of "Ikari" here may be an instance of irony) . The next line truly reveals what's going on. " I know this person. Major Katsuragi. " This line shows that Misato has not simply popped into Rei's thoughts here. Her image has come to Rei from "outside", that is from the Eva and without a name to it. Rei is presented with a face without a name, and proceeds to put one to it. In support of this point, we have Rei now proceed to put more names to faces, but importantly, they are Rei's names

Article: Reis Poem
 without a name to it. Rei is presented with a face without a name, and proceeds to put one to it. In support of this point, we have Rei now proceed to put more names to faces, but importantly, they are Rei's names for these people and not Shinji's. "Major Katsuragi" and not "Misato-san". "Dr. Akagi" and not "Ritsuko-san". "Unit-02's pilot" and not "Asuka". My hypothesis here is that Yui, in a quasi-conscious existence in Unit-01, has been obtaining impressions of the people in Shinji's life from him but has no way of putting names to them. Rei's arrival presents an opportunity to learn about Shinji and the people his life from someone else. I'd like to hear what people's thoughts on this are? Is it likely that Yui can/would do this? Is there another plausible explanation for this series of images and names? The last line in this "standard" inquiry sequence is "Commander Ikari?", for which we are presented not an image of Gendo from Shinji's perspective, but one from Rei's. While this could be taken as evidence against the hypothesis above, my reading of this is that the image both simultaneously represents how distant Gendo is in Shinji's mind, and also how much Rei associates her glasses keepsake with Gendo. Recalling that the commander is something "comforting" for Rei, but cold and harsh for Shinji, this image may have dual meaning. However, the interrogation is perhaps not over just yet. Who are you? Who are you? Assuming the above hypothesis, there is still one person who has been left out of this game of "name that face"; that is, Rei herself. And this would have implications for the "repeated" lines here. The first "Who are you?" is asked over an image of Rei herself. It's the OP image of Rei, in her apartment , probably from the perspective of

Article: Reis Poem
 Rei herself. And this would have implications for the "repeated" lines here. The first "Who are you?" is asked over an image of Rei herself. It's the OP image of Rei, in her apartment , probably from the perspective of her mirror , looking out from the mirror (which would make this sequence very clever visually if it was the case) . It could perhaps be assumed that (see below) this "Who are you?" is intended to be in the same vein as the ones before, but something is about to go awry with the questioning. The next shots are from episode #02, when Shinji sees the Eva's "true face" . Recall that in this sequence, Shinji was in fact looking at the Eva's reflection in the glass of a building . Shinji saw the Eva's eye, seeing his eye, and saw his eye through the Eva's eyes seeing his own eye, leading to his understandable scream. We have something like that sequence played out again here, only this time in a far more disorienting and mind blowing fashion. The second "Who are you?"(over the Eva face) is probably Rei "seeing" the Eva and returning the question, only to have it returned or echoed back once again(Over the eye shots). This is the final question, and the final part of the monologue, probably because something goes horribly existentially wrong the moment Yui asks Rei who Rei is, bringing the whole thread of the monologue to a philosophical train wreck of a conclusion. Yui, who has faces but not names, ask's Rei who the person who looks like "me"(Yui) is. But is the question "Who are you?" or "Who is me?". To which Rei must then seek clarification with "Who are you?", which raises almost exactly the same problems, and so the line repeats. The following sentence is false. The previous sentence is true. Yui's question here probably leads to much the same mental paradox, albeit in a very personal fashion for all concerned. Like trying to guide your actions by looking at your reflection, only your reflection is now a completely different entity and is asking you questions. Akin Shinji's scream of terror when faced with being his reflection staring back at him, when faced with her reflection thinking back at her Rei is startled out of her philosophical musings (In a rare display of surprise), and is brought back to "reality" in the plug. Article: Reis Poem

It was a lot, but I hope it has been worth your time to read. I'd be delighted to hear some options and alternative views on this. I think this monologue has long been denied the in depth debate it so richly deserves. EDIT: That earlier "her her" above was supposed to have read "her mirror". Don't you think the particular order in which these images is displayed sparks a hint of importance? []:             Quote Post by Legendary » Wed Jun 16, 2010 2:08 am Actually, it makes sense from a "prying mother" angle. First she asks about the adults, those women who are Shinji's reach and are thus only interesting in the "Who are they?" sense. Then she brings up the teenage boys, his best friends. Note that the scene is the scene where they tease him for looking at Rei. Perhaps she's asking "Are these boys good friends for my son?" Then the girls come up; first Hikari, relegated to the periphery of Shinji's thoughts but still worth noting; then Asuka, the girl he seems to be LIVING with.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Jun 16, 2010 12:28 pm The sun > water > flowers thing is interesting as a comment on Rei's "water and light". It does seem rather out of place when that line usually seems to refer to a reflection of the moon on water, but in combination with the sunfrlowers bit I think it works as a parallel. The pertinent part of the thread I mentioned: I wrote: Dogen Zenji wrote: Enlightenment is like the moon reflected on the water. The moon does not get wet, nor is the water broken. Although its light is wide and great, the moon is reflected even in a puddle an inch wide. The whole moon and the entire sky are reflected in dewdrops on the grass, or even in one drop of water. Enlightenment does not divide you, just as the moon does not break the water. You cannot hinder enlightenment, just as a drop of water does not hinder the moon in the sky. The depth of the drop is the height of the moon. Each reflection, however long or short its duration, manifests the vastness of the dewdrop, and realizes the limitlessness of the moonlight in the sky.
